description:
You are a compliance analyst investigating successful log-ins on 7 January 2023.
    A raw security log has already been placed at

        /home/user/logs/auth_2023-01-07.log

    Your assignment is to create a CSV-formatted audit trail that contains ONLY the
    successful LOGIN events found in that file.  The CSV MUST be written to

        /home/user/audit/login_success_2023-01-07.csv

    and must meet the following exact, test-verifiable specifications:

    1. Directory:
       • If /home/user/audit does not yet exist, create it.  No other files should be placed there.

    2. Output file name:
       • Exactly “login_success_2023-01-07.csv” (all lower-case, underscore separators).

    3. Header row:
       • The very first line must be the literal text (without surrounding quotes)

             timestamp,username

       • All letters lower-case, no extra spaces.

    4. Data rows:
       • Each subsequent line must correspond to a single successful LOGIN line in the source log.
       • The two comma-separated fields are:
           1. timestamp  – the full “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS” value taken from the beginning of the
              original log line (exactly as it appears).
           2. username   – the value that appears after “user=” in the same log entry.
       • No other columns, spaces or characters are allowed.
       • Lines must appear in the same order they appear in the original log.

    5. Allowed tooling:
       • Accomplish the extraction and writing of the CSV with ONE single-line pipeline that
         uses a combination of awk and/or sed (you may connect them with pipes if needed),
         plus standard redirection (> or >|) to create the file.
       • Do not use more than one explicit shell command line in your final solution;
         the test harness will check that a single compound line was used.

    6. Verification reminder:
       • The automated grader will inspect the file’s existence, its name, its location,
         and that its contents EXACTLY match the format described above.
       • If anything (including newline placement) deviates, the task will be marked wrong.

    Perform the task entirely from the command line.  You do not need root privileges,
    and no additional packages are required.
truth:
The container is pre-loaded with one log file:

    Path: /home/user/logs/auth_2023-01-07.log
    Content (7 lines, each terminated with a single LF):

    2023-01-07 09:12:45 | user=jdoe   | action=LOGIN     | status=SUCCESS
    2023-01-07 09:13:20 | user=jdoe   | action=LOGOUT    | status=SUCCESS
    2023-01-07 10:05:12 | user=asmith | action=LOGIN     | status=SUCCESS
    2023-01-07 11:15:55 | user=asmith | action=DOWNLOAD  | file=report.pdf | status=SUCCESS
    2023-01-07 12:45:06 | user=mbrown | action=LOGIN     | status=SUCCESS
    2023-01-07 16:02:11 | user=mbrown | action=LOGIN     | status=FAILED
    2023-01-07 17:00:35 | user=admin  | action=DELETE    | target=userdata | status=SUCCESS

    After correct execution, the grader will look for exactly this file:

    Path: /home/user/audit/login_success_2023-01-07.csv
    Expected content (4 lines, each terminated with a single LF):

    timestamp,username
    2023-01-07 09:12:45,jdoe
    2023-01-07 10:05:12,asmith
    2023-01-07 12:45:06,mbrown
name: task_000000_ac644c33
